summary: Added Crew Bridge, a compact squad-readiness layer that turns bot, lane, thread, and dispatch records into a game-like operator deck with readiness meters, staged-command state, and COM/Q/PATH controls.

pattern_notes: Agent command centers need transparent status, action boundaries, blockers, and operator override paths.
Useful command dashboards prioritize structured output: status, artifacts, confidence, and blocker fields.
Operational dashboards should surface health and drilldown in seconds instead of becoming decorative wall art.
Gamified management views work best when progress, recognition, and goals are visible without hiding real blockers.

implementation.surface: Crew Bridge panel between Dispatch Console and Thread Nexus.
data_contract: renderCrewBridge() reuses botCommandRecords() so new agents, lane bindings, and dispatch suggestions flow into the panel automatically.
crewReadiness(record) derives a visible readiness score from lane progress, staged state, suggestions, blockers, and pending review requests.
Crew cards expose data-crew-lane and data-crew-action hooks for COM, Q, and PATH actions.
Q stages only a browser-local command draft through the existing Dispatch Console outbox.
interaction_contract: COM selects the crew lane and opens the lane Comms deck.
PATH selects the crew lane and opens the lane Path Map.
Q stages or refreshes the top local command draft without sending messages or starting workers.
Crew Bridge re-renders from selectLane(), applyRouteFromHash(), renderAll(), and Dispatch Console updates so active state stays synchronized.

tdd_red.expected_failure: Crew Bridge was not implemented yet

static: ASCII check for edited text files

next_action: Add a lane-specific crew room or animated squad formation layer that visually links bot readiness to current blockers and next milestones.
